title: ericameria viscidiflora subsp. planifolia
intro: Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. planifolia, also known as the Bigelow's greasewood, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Bigelow's greasewood include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to protect against infections and promote healing. Bigelow's greasewood can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the latter being particularly effective for topical applications.
